_meta.purpose: Analyst-stated CAUSAL reasons for R1 ranks — feeds matcher premium tags, not picks

why_high: Chess-piece versatility — Ohio State aligned him off-ball LB, on-edge rusher, and QB spy in same game
Elite testing: 4.46 forty at 244 lbs tied fastest LB at the combine
Rare length/size combo — 6-5, 244 with closing burst of an edge rusher
Young prospect (DOB 8/30/2005), turns 21 August; Micah Parsons / Owusu-Koramoah comp grades
Two-steps-ahead mental processing & recognition stack up with best LBs in recent years
Inexperienced but high football IQ — developmental ceiling uncommonly high
Arvell Reese.primary_analyst_quote: Jeremiah: ranked No. 5 overall Top-50 v4.0 — 'chess piece'; Brugler Top-100 No. 1 — 'most physically impressive LB in class, closing burst of an edge rusher'
Arvell Reese.floor_concern: Only 1 sack in 2025 stat line, inexperienced as full-time starter

why_high: Route-running technician — runs every branch of the route tree, manipulates DBs at stem
Soft reliable hands — plucks away from frame, best hands in WR class
Large catch radius + body control — 'The Great Adjuster', wins 50/50 balls
Proven X-WR profile — sideline wins, contested catches, true boundary receiver at 6-2 3/4
George Pickens comp with better hands — OROY upside per CBS
Smart coverage-reader — understands route concepts and defensive structures
Carnell Tate.primary_analyst_quote: CBS Sports: 'George Pickens-type skill set but with better hands'; Brugler/Jeremiah grade as top-10 WR1
Carnell Tate.floor_concern: Average separation speed — not a true burner, 875 yds on 51 catches suggests volume not dominance

why_high: Best raw strength in the EDGE class — puts lone blockers on skates, holds up vs doubles
Twitchy explosiveness despite compact 6-2, 263 stocky build
Integrated hands/footwork — masterful stutter and euro-step pacing manipulation
Three-year production: 24 TFL / 14 sacks over past 2+ seasons, ACC DROY freshman
PFF rates him EDGE1 in class — power-rusher archetype that translates immediately
Run defense floor — elite POA anchor rare for edge
Rueben Bain.primary_analyst_quote: PFF: 'the No. 1 EDGE defender in the 2026 NFL Draft'; CBS: 'plays like a potential No. 1 pick' despite non-prototype build
Rueben Bain.floor_concern: Arm length bottom 1% for EDGE — will fall below most teams' length thresholds; tackle radius limited

why_high: Top pure guard in class — clear G1, likely first IOL off the board
Elite POA power — halts bull rushes, creates displacement, compresses LOS
Pass pro dominance: 0 sacks, 0 QB hits, 4 pressures allowed in 2025
Firm anchor with patience & balance — tone-setter run blocker in gap/zone
Two-year starter at Penn State LG, All-Big Ten 2nd team
Plug-and-play Week 1 starter floor — lowest-bust-risk IOL
Olaivavega Ioane.primary_analyst_quote: Brugler/PFF: 'clear top interior offensive line prospect in the 2026 class'; B/R: 'strong candidate to be first guard selected'
Olaivavega Ioane.floor_concern: Below-average arms (32 3/4) and movement skills — struggles in space vs quicker 3-techs

why_high: Record-breaking athlete — 4.39 forty at 241 lbs shattered all-time TE combine record
Rare dual-skill TE — elite in-line blocker AND receiving threat, not a move-TE only
Explosive YAC + 3-level receiving threat with first-round WR speed
Tenacious physical blocker — dominates off-ball LBs, can match up with edge defenders
Consensus TE1 — Big Ten Kwalick-Clark TE of the Year, 560 yds / 8 TDs as junior
Positional scarcity — TE1 in weak TE class, no other Day-1 TE threatens his grade
Kenyon Sadiq.primary_analyst_quote: PFF / Brugler: 'consensus TE1'; Ravens scout comp to Mark Andrews; 'rare explosiveness at the tight end position'
Kenyon Sadiq.floor_concern: 10.5% drop rate, inconsistent catch mechanics, raw route-running nuance

why_high: Rare centerfield range — 'arguably the best range in the class' patrolling middle third
Prototype size at safety — 6-3, 201 with 4.52 speed, length uncommon for position
Elite production: 206 tackles, 13 PBUs, 5 INTs, 8 FFs across 35 starts
Physical tone-setter — downhill trigger + hitting mentality
Three-year starter ball production — FF/INT combo scouts love
Small-school riser — Toledo tape evaluates like Power-5 first-rounder
Emmanuel McNeil-Warren.primary_analyst_quote: Ringer/Bleacher Report: 'true centerfielder with arguably the best range in the class'; Yahoo: top-25 first-rounder
Emmanuel McNeil-Warren.floor_concern: Stiff hips, limited man-coverage utility vs WRs/TEs, long speed questions

why_high: Elite YAC / run-after-catch creator — among college football's most explosive with ball in hands
Expansive route tree — proven three-level receiver, not gadget-limited anymore
Breakout Texas A&M season reset narrative from 'gimmick' to 'first-rounder'
Development arc: 3-star recruit to All-American — ascending trajectory
Scheme-versatile — wins from slot, motion, jet sweeps, outside
Dynamic do-it-all weapon fit in modern NFL spread offenses
KC Concepcion.primary_analyst_quote: Bleacher Report: 'fits the mold as a dynamic do-it-all, run-after-catch receiver in today's NFL'; Rams prospect preview: late-R1 grade
KC Concepcion.floor_concern: Focus drops, some teams pigeonhole as slot-only, sub-6-foot height

why_high: Top DT prospect in class — consensus DT1 in a down interior-DL year
Elite get-off / first-step explosion — wins leverage before blockers react
Powerful POA anchor — rarely moved in run game, locks down interior gaps
Young prospect — will be 21 at draft, 3-year starter as 4-star recruit
Synced hands/feet — sheds blocks quickly, high technical ceiling
High upside with frame still filling out — developmental ceiling as pass rusher
Peter Woods.primary_analyst_quote: B/R: 'top defensive tackle prospect in the 2026 NFL draft class'; Walter Football: high upside, 21 at draft
Peter Woods.floor_concern: Short arms (31.25), inconsistent pad level, limited pass-rush plan/win rate — more run-stuffer than disruptor

why_high: Elite physical tools — 6-5 7/8, 321 lbs, 33 7/8 arms, 4.91 forty, 25 bench reps
Twitchy pass-set explosiveness — 'small, twitchy movement skills of top NFL tackles'
Massive ceiling / 'mystery box' — raw traits evaluators project to elite starter
Zone-scheme fit — athletic profile perfect for outside-zone offenses
Late-bloomer story — didn't play football until 2021, pure trait bet
Nigerian immigrant, basketball/soccer background drives athletic fluidity
Max Iheanachor.primary_analyst_quote: PFF: 'ceiling through the roof'; Yahoo: 'has every physical tool in the book'; Packers reportedly eyeing him late R1
Max Iheanachor.floor_concern: Lower floor than peers — technical inconsistency, pitter-patter pass sets, speed-to-power gets him on heels
